title: Lifeblood
author: MintFace
excerpt: Exploring the world in high resolution shows the art of Planet Earth in ways that were shrouded for most of the 20th Century. Intrepid brings those impossible landscapes to the blockchain.

title: Tokenized Photographs
author: MintFace
excerpt: Why tokenize a photo? Because photographs are worth saving. They are a form of visual history that shows who we were at that time.

title: Talking About Artists
author: MintFace
excerpt: Eric Bleicher (Line 312) updated his Line. Three years of growth, three surfaces for collectors — and a masterclass in artist presence.

title: The Line is Never Finished
author: MintFace
excerpt: "The Line is never finished" has been a catchphrase of the community since 2022. As marketplaces shutter, The Line steps up a gear.

title: The End of Gatekeepers
author: theline1
excerpt: Don't Wait For Permission You can mint an artwork and sell it to anyone in the world without a gallery. You don't need to wait for permission. The media…

title: Roads and Rivers Exhibition Explores Connection
author: theline1
excerpt: The Artist: MintFace MintFace said he chose then to photograph the bridges that he said were the most beautiful or rarely seen. MintFace said most of the…

title: The Line Gallery in New Zealand Opens
author: theline1
excerpt: Tokenised Art for the People The first art show at The Line is now open to the public. Launched by owner and founder of The Line, the artist known as MintFace.…

title: Art Drought Declared on 6529 Network
author: theline1
excerpt: Official Art Drought Declared The level of art on the 6529 Network has reached alarming levels so an official 'Art Drought' has been declared. Officials are…

author: theline1
excerpt:
​👨‍🏫 5 Course Modules ​

2. Connect Vault To Inscribing Wallet
3. ​Fund Inscribing Wallet with BTC
4. ​Inscribe Artist Token ​
5. Inscribe Your Collection on BTC

title: Punks with Lore
author: theline1
excerpt: Open Call for Punks with Lore We are looking for the top 69 Punks with lore. A story that is so memorable, hilarious or downright eye watering...that it must…

title: CryptoCubes with Han
author: theline1
excerpt: HanRGB is a Turkish designer and generative artist best known for creating CryptoCubes, a collection of 256 unique digital landmarks that blend conceptual minimalism with high-tech aesthetics. His work puts the sculptures of the future on-chain, making their relevance permanently accessible. Virtual architecture is celebrated at The Line and we host a custom CryptoCube in the lobby of The Line.

title: Rowhomes with Chris Hytha
author: theline1
excerpt: The Artist: Chris Hytha Commitment to your craft is acquiring a Rowhome after released 100 photographs in a collection with the same title. Chris Hytha, a Philly-based photographer is renowned for his ‘Rowhomes’ series, which captures the unique character of the city’s historic row houses.

title: Surpassing Death with Zoe Louise
author: theline1
excerpt: Zoe Louise articulates the untouchable through painting that leaves the paint ‘brush’ of bloomed roses on the canvas. Her digital prints on Ordinals lets the light bathe the surface of the artwork.  Polarity by Zoe Louise on Ordinals “I was wondering if reality could surpass death.

title: Superflat with Will A.K.
author: theline1
excerpt: Will is short for Willhelm Ardylles Kurniawan or willak.eth for his collectoors. An Indonesian autodidact who basically lives on a highway of superflat aesthetics, fiction-as-life, and absurdist vibes. Will pulls from the superflat tradition but makes it way messier—less polished, more personal. The dude’s whole thing is turning the banal into a caricature of itself, so it loops back into feeling meaningful again (maybe).

title: Crushing Life with Jubbish
author: theline1
excerpt: Jubbish (aka jay) is a lens based artist who thrives in the paradoxical space between chaos and precision. His work seems like a riff on the absurd, flipping the banal into visual puns and abstract commentary that feels oddly resonant with internet-native culture. He’s got a knack for making the deeply surreal feel approachable, balancing a lo-fi aesthetic with high-concept undertones.

title: Neo-Precisionism with Grant Yun
author: theline1
excerpt: Grant Yun is a crypto artist known for blending sweet pastel palette landscapes with nostalgic American vibes, often informed by his medical precision and appreciation for rural and suburban settings. His works emphasize simplicity. A sense of calm and reflection wash over you. Yun's art has resonated widely in the cryptoart space. tl:dr David Hockney wif tokens.

title: Grifter or Remnants with XCOPY
author: theline1
excerpt: Something changed when XCOPY released the happy, cheery summer.jpg in August 2021...and something changed again when XCOPY released Remnants. Inside you are two wolves. One wolf, gets the inside joke of using a 1 of 1 Grifter as your profile picture. The other, realises that Remnants is perhaps the most distilled voice of XCOPY to date. Which wolf are you?

title: Apocalypse Now with apocalypse
author: theline1
excerpt: World building is often thrown around in cryptoart yet few have the patience to bring it to life over the multiple years it takes. Apocalypse is the exception. Read the 'On The Run' screenplay for the 2025 short film...that is the crescendo for a release of characters over the last three years 2021-2024.

title: Shades of Gabriella Morton
author: theline1
excerpt: The secret to Gabriella Morton's success is refined conceptualisation of an idea. Each artwork stands as evidence of her wrestling with the potential of scene, saying and shared experience. Klutch, Grant Shannon, Shalin and Norcal Guy have also recognized the immense future potential that Gabriella is hinting at.

title: Portugal Awakening with andginja
author: theline1
excerpt: The Artist: andginja Andginja is a talented photographer and visual artist based in Lisbon, known for capturing the vibrant essence of urban life and architecture. His photography collection from Lisbon highlights the city’s unique charm, blending traditional Portuguese aesthetics with modern perspectives.

title: Maxxed out with MAX CAPACITY
author: theline1
excerpt: The name of MAX CAPACITY's bio photo is crime+breakfast.png, denoting that the memetic fun runs deeper than surface level. The fetishization of obsolete tech gets the decayed frames rolling. MAX CAPACITY has played with the full breadth of Internet tools since 2008, literally grandpa vibes in terms of the crypto glitch kiddies.

title: Beyond Manic
author: theline1
excerpt: The Artist: MaNiC Art MaNiC Art creates his own reality through art, to illuminate the climb towards his crypto dreams. Enhanced creativity and expression is made possible in times of heightened energy… with the themes expressed in MaNiC’s art often drawing on the low energy times.

title: Blank Characters with gingerpotter
author: theline1
excerpt: Gingerpotter explores what it means to co-exist in both the digital and physical world. Through 'blank characters' we introduced to human moments experienced in either the digital or physical world. 'We are trying to understand who to be in the digital world and how to be in the physical world.' says gingerpotter, 'everything might be bigger than the individual but the individual stands out.

title: Symptoms of Yellow
author: theline1
excerpt: DocT's creative inspiration began with journalistic writing and shifted towards photojournalism while stationed in the Middle East as part of his medical studies. DocT self prescribes making art as a way to handle the day job as a Doctor helping kids. Shades of yellow keep appearing across the 17 collections DocT has released to date. He asks the viewer to decide whether the yellow you are seeing is one of joy and happiness or warning and caution.

title: Coding Rhythm with Dmitri Cherniak
author: theline1
excerpt: If generative artists are the conductors of an orchestra of code, then Dmitri is placed at the very height of long form symphony. Having attained a sale price of 2,100 ETH for Ringers #109 and 1,800 ETH for Ringers #879 (aka The Goose), one could mistakenly overlook his art beyond the headline fame.

title: Class of 2021. Active in 2024.
author: theline1
excerpt: The Class of 2021 Artists who minted their first work in 2021… or were minting art for years earlier are a strong signal of OG status. Collectoors who acquired art from the OGs are probably wondering…are they still around? MintFace has done the hard work so you can check.

title: Minted Music Artists
author: theline1
excerpt: I know almost nothing about music NFTs so asked on X…and boy MintFace got schooled. A list of artists mentioned is below or read the original thread. Stefanie Neal A London born, Sydney based, self-taught mixed media artist who has been honing her creativity since childhood. Introduced to NFTs in October 2021.

title: Sharing Burdens
author: theline1
excerpt: MintFace is a Geodetic Reality Futurist who lays down a path for another person to one day pick up on. The image-laden pathway creates a legacy... and in doing so, becomes a way to understand the grit of the path less travelled, for those who choose it or it chooses them.

title: Future Nostalgia with Matty Furious
author: theline1
excerpt: The Artist: Matty Furious Matty Furious joined SuperRare in November 2018, one of the first 50 artists to mint there. Matty's art heralds the tokenized echoes…

title: Absurd Suburbia with Ben Zank
author: theline1
excerpt: Unorthodoxy thrives in the suburban streets turned theatrestage by Ben Zank. The suited and booted protagonist is found contorted in and around the surfaces of suburban lives that 99.999% of the rest of the residents have never considered, let alone attempted. That is what makes Zank's work sizzle. We are forced to ask ourselves 'what is happening here?' Tans, yellows, oranges and pine green are a recurring palette across many of Zank's work.

title: Twin Outtakes with Justin Aversano
author: theline1
excerpt: Connecting humans together are the three words that speak most clearly when I hear the name Justin Aversano. He travels, spends time with the subjects of his portraits, and when the feeling takes him, snaps the shutter and preserves the proof on the blockchain. Aversano has inspired many photographers since Twin Flames to mint a well rounded, large collection of photographs that say more as a collection than independently.

title: Neon Divinity with Dangiuz
author: theline1
excerpt: Romantic Cyberpunk artist Danguiz, hurtles us into the future to ponder what it might mean to exist in the 22nd Century and beyond. With Blade Runner four decades in an our rear vision mirror, many dystopian aspects of its forecast have already come to pass. Yet there is hope within the scenes Dangiuz creates. The cat reminds us that we still care for the animals, despite an inundation of super-sized pixel displays.

title: Double Happy with Gloomtube
author: theline1
excerpt: The ironic smiley faced leader of gloom began on June 22nd 2021 with the release of 'Brutalized Police Car.' The rebellious spirit has continued through the release of the collection titled 'Gloomerican Dreams' with 489 co-conspirators participating in the on-chain riot broadcast on Gloomtube.

title: Nomad Art with Yongoh Kim
author: theline1
excerpt: Yongoh Kim represents a relatively recent phenomenon of available to cryptoartists live nomadic lives, earning crypto for the art to fund their journey's.

title: The Death of Color with Cowboy Killer
author: theline1
excerpt: A sense of stillness is conveyed through how light dapples and composition directs photographed subject matter that questions our very urbane existence. The color in Cowboy Killers work is drained out of existence and what is left is a scene of apologetic paralysis.

title: The Genesis of Generative with DCA
author: theline1
excerpt: The Artist: Daniel Calderon (DCA) The beginning of generative art on Art Blocks started on Day Zero. The first day of minting art as crispy ERC-721 tokens on…

title: Writing Pictures with Vinnie Hager
author: theline1
excerpt: Vinne Hager has refined his visual language into a series of symbols derived from a master set of 100 diary drawings made over a period of a decade. In these drawings lay the clues to a newfound strength to grow through art. Unlike the outward protest symbols present of Keith Haring’s work, Hagers symbols to date are more introspective.

title: Historical Proof with Photogurapher
author: theline1
excerpt: The content explores the intersection of photography and AI-driven 'post-photography'. It raises questions about the erosion of truth in digital imagery, postulating on alternate histories in parallel universes. Through artist Photogurapher, themes of reinvented realities emerge, challenging perceptions of art and emotion in the evolving landscape of AI art.

title: Mostly Implicit with hisartwork
author: theline1
excerpt: The artist known as hisartwork minted the 'Birth of Ethereum' token in 2018, selling it for 0.2 eth. Despite unsuccessful attempts to repurchase it for 1 eth, he has created over 2,177 additional artworks. His 'Unconscious Collage' collection emphasizes open-source creativity, allowing public remixing and reuse. His piece 'Tantrums' highlights personal expression.

title: Crypto = 1 Dick and 1 Butt
author: theline1
excerpt: CryptoDickButts (CDB), created by K.C. Green in 2006, is a satirical digital art project that has become a cultural phenomenon within the cryptocurrency community. The collection features 5,200 unique artworks. CDB challenges traditional art norms and appeals to crypto enthusiasts, highlighting the playful absurdity inherent in internet culture.

title: Toco Deco and the Machine
author: theline1
excerpt: Toco Deco is a distinguished Latin American artist noted for her expressive minimalist artworks that merge digital and traditional techniques. Her pieces explore abstract concepts and liminal spaces, exemplified by works like "Beyond The Horizon." The collection "Halcyon" reflects nostalgic themes, questioning AI's interpretation of earlier digital realities.

title: Meridian with Matt DesLauriers
author: theline1
excerpt: The Artist: Matt DesLauriers Matt DesLauriers is a Canadian-born generative artist and creative coder based in London. His work primarily focuses on code,…

title: Permanent Decay with Andy Schwetz
author: theline1
excerpt: The Artist: Andy Schwetz Andy Schwetz is a fine art and documentary photographer based in Munich, Germany. His work delves into the melancholy of the past,…

title: Thirst for Bad with Terrell Dom
author: theline1
excerpt: The Artist: Terrell Dom Terrell Dom exemplifies a new kind of Pop-Precisionism by combining the structured, clean lines of Precisionism with the vibrant,…

title: The Kandinsky of Crypto
author: theline1
excerpt: The Artist: Guillermo Arismendi Guillermo Arismendi paints with both oil and digital mediums, infusing boundless energy into each practice. The abstract artist…

title: Adventures with TrailMix
author: theline1
excerpt: The Artist: TrailMix With the expansive natural beauty of Boulder, Colorado at his backdoor, TrailMix finds his inspiration on the paths of nature. Once the…

title: Isolated Together with Amy Elkins
author: theline1
excerpt: Amy Elkins is a contemporary artist and photographer who turned inwards during the Pandemic with her conceptual approach to portraiture and the human condition during the lockdowns. Elkins uses cyanotype processing, a simple 19th Century technique that remained possible in a world without retail. Her thoughtful engagement with time during the lockdown, showing vulnerability with one Anxious Pleasure photograph shared a day.

title: Soul to Bare with Ayla el Moussa
author: theline1
excerpt: Ayla El Moussa is a celebrated crypto artist known for her evocative imagery that maintains a distance between the viewer and the subject. Her collections, including "Nude Pixels" and "Scene 1," explore themes of feminine form through layered perspectives, inviting viewers to engage with the art's depth and mystery.

title: Tranquil Terrain with mae
author: theline1
excerpt: Mae is an artist known for her minimalistic and romantic approach to landscape art, blending realism and surrealism. Since joining the cryptoart scene in 2021, she has created "Postcards by Mae," a digital collection reflecting her artistic journey, featuring tranquil landscapes that evoke a sense of nostalgia and peace.

title: Vague Scenes with David Loh
author: theline1
excerpt: David Loh, a cryptoart artist since March 2022, explores the significance of overlooked spaces in his collection "Aesthetics of the Immaterial." His works, such as "aesrial #15 'high life'," confront viewers with simplicity and engage them in reflecting on the deeper meanings behind seemingly mundane environments, blending absurdity with insight.

title: Delicate Perception with dubhz0
author: theline1
excerpt: Dubhz0 is a crypto artist merging photography and AI, exploring minimalism through their collection "Liminal Emptiness". The artwork, particularly "Liminal Emptiness #17", challenges perceptions of reality versus artificiality, emphasizing AI's subtlety and its complex role in art. The collection evokes familiar yet otherworldly transitional spaces.

title: The Duality of Life with R0YA
author: theline1
excerpt: Roya explores the duality of life through her artwork, juxtaposing inner feelings with external perceptions. Her creations, often featuring symbolic motifs like butterflies and flowing hair, convey powerful emotions and identity searches. Notable works like 'A Warrior's Soul' and 'Mahsa Amini' combine vulnerability with political statements, pushing art into transformative realms.

title: Phases of Art with Loren Bednar
author: theline1
excerpt: The Phase collection is a visual exploration of interference patterns, showcasing dynamic, colorful displays that evoke motion and energy. It features varying pixel resolutions, distinct scanline variants, and diverse color palettes. Released in August 2021 amidst an art market boom, it encourages large-scale exhibitions to enhance viewer experience.

title: Saving Virtual Souls with Claire Silver
author: theline1
excerpt: Claire Silver is an innovative artist in the AI art movement, known for her collections such as 'Genesis' and 'Pixelgeist.' While she faces criticism, her work explores the intersection of traditional portraiture and AI. Pixelgeist showcases 2,136 pieces, emphasizing accessibility and the complexities of AI-generated art, particularly in piece #986.

title: Fast Money with Damien Hirst
author: theline1
excerpt: Damien Hirst, a prominent contemporary artist, challenges traditional art values through his collection "The Currency," launched in July 2021. It features 10,000 hand-painted pieces competing with NFTs, forcing collectors to choose between physical and digital ownership. This experiment highlights evolving perceptions of value in the digital era and Hirst's legacy in art.

title: In a Heartbeat with Drifter Shoots
author: theline1
excerpt: Drifter Shoots, the artistic alias of Isaac Wright, is a renowned photographer whose work showcases urban landscapes and human resilience. His iconic "Where My Vans Go" collection merges personal narratives with stunning visuals, symbolizing the fragility and strength of the human experience. His piece "Heartbeat" epitomizes freedom in cryptoart.

title: Generative Cryptoarte with Seb Broucher
author: theline1
excerpt: Cryptoarte, created by Sebastián Broucher, features 10,000 generative artworks, with highlights including unique paintings associated with prime numbers and mints predating Autoglyphs. Each painting illustrates Ethereum network activity through color coding. Notably, commercial rights accompany the artworks, allowing for resale in various forms, as exemplified by its inclusion in the MINTED book.

title: Jungle Bay Island
author: theline1
excerpt: Jungle Bay Island A fresh movement in cryptoart has begun elevating artists AND cutting them in not out of 100% of sales revenue. The last meme contest dished…

title: Up Close with Matt Doogue
author: theline1
excerpt: Matt Doogue captures the intricate lives of tiny creatures through macro photography, demonstrating resilience in nature. His work, praised for its technical quality and deeper messages, has featured in esteemed publications and galleries. Notable pieces include "Same Storm, Different Boats," portraying struggle and persistence in both art and life.

author: theline1
excerpt: Fine Art Photographer 4 SOLD OUT NFT Collections.

author: theline1
excerpt: All Time High World is a collective of artists who fly high to reveal our planet in all its brilliance. Each artist flies high in search of a composition made…

author: theline1
excerpt: The XLIFE collection by MintFace documents the machinations of memorable crypto moments through the lens of XCOPY. On The Line…

title: Seize and Share Memes 3
author: theline1
excerpt: This Seize and Share Memes article continues the deep dive on the Seize and Share collection Part 1 by MintFace revealing the narratives behind the seized and shared memes released over the last six months. Each meme was seized and shared permission-lessly thanks to CC0; the most permissive of all intellectual property copyrights.

title: Conceptual Photography
author: theline1
excerpt:
Conceptual photography expresses an idea or concept through photos. Specifically, the idea is fundamental to the processing of creating the photograph in the first place.

The Roads and Rivers collection expresses two big ideas that are broadly relatable to all of us, and one personal idea that is only known by the artist and to collectors who ask. That is the beauty of conceptual photography... it can say many things.

title: Seize and Share Memes
author: theline1
excerpt:
This article is a deep dive on the Seize and Share collection by MintFace revealing the narratives behind the seized and shared memes released over the last six months. Each meme was seized and shared permission-lessly thanks to CC0; the most permissive of all intellectual property copyrights.

Seize and Share collection is the most comprehensive set of memes by a single artist, minted often within hours of the original meme.
